text: Adam and Eve had a son named Cain, and Eve said she had gotten a man with the LORD's help.
She also bore Abel; Abel became a shepherd of flocks, and Cain worked the soil.
In time Cain brought some of his crops as an offering to the LORD.
Abel brought the best portions of his firstborn flock, and the LORD looked with favor on Abel and his gift.
But he had no regard for Cain's offering, and Cain grew furious and downcast.
The LORD asked Cain why he was angry and downcast.
He warned Cain that sin was crouching at his door, desiring him, but he must master it.
Cain spoke to his brother Abel, and out in the field Cain attacked and killed him.
The LORD asked Cain where his brother was, and Cain said he didn't know — was he his brother's keeper?
The LORD said Abel's blood was crying out to him from the ground.
Cain was now cursed, driven from the ground that had drunk his brother's blood.
The ground would no longer yield its strength to him, and he would be a restless wanderer.
Cain said his punishment was more than he could bear.
He said he would be driven from the land and from God's presence, and anyone who found him would kill him.
The LORD promised sevenfold vengeance on anyone who killed Cain, and put a mark on him so no one would.
Cain left the LORD's presence and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
Cain's wife bore Enoch, and Cain built a city, naming it after his son.
Enoch fathered Irad, who fathered Mehujael, who fathered Methushael, who fathered Lamech.
Lamech took two wives, Adah and Zillah.
Adah bore Jabal, father of those who live in tents and raise livestock.
His brother was Jubal, father of all who play the harp and flute.
Zillah bore Tubal-Cain, who forged tools of bronze and iron, and his sister was Naamah.
Lamech boasted to his wives that he killed a man for wounding him, a young man for striking him.
He claimed if Cain was avenged sevenfold, he would be avenged seventy-sevenfold.
Adam and Eve had another son, Seth, whom Eve saw as a replacement for Abel.
Seth also had a son, Enosh; at that time people began to call on the name of the LORD.
